clues.clue: This chain's 1948 'Speedee Service System' pioneered assembly-line fast-food cooking.
Colonel Sanders first sold his fried chicken from a service station in Corbin, in this state.
This chain opened its first drive-thru-only stand in 1948 in Baldwin Park, California.
Founded in 1958 by the Carney brothers in Wichita, this chain is named for its small original building.
This coffee chain opened its first store at Seattle's Pike Place Market in 1971.
This German company introduced the original gummy bear in the 1920s.
These chewy fruit squares launched in the UK in 1960 as 'Opal Fruits.'
This Hershey brand of red, twisted licorice-style ropes is a chewy staple.
This pulled, pastel boardwalk candy contains little to none of the sea water in its name.
Many sour candies are dusted with citric acid and this other fruit acid found in green apples.
'They're gr-r-reat!' is the long-time catchphrase of this tiger-fronted cereal.
'Snap! Crackle! Pop!' are the three elf mascots of this crisped-rice cereal.
'The Breakfast of Champions' is the slogan of this athlete-endorsed wheat-flake cereal.
'Follow your nose' is Toucan Sam's motto for this fruity-ring cereal.
'Kid tested, mother approved' has long advertised this General Mills cereal.
Alexander Fleming discovered this antibiotic from Penicillium mold in 1928.
Jonas Salk developed the first widely used vaccine against this paralytic disease.
This Austrian friar's pea-plant experiments founded the science of genetics.
Watson and Crick described the double-helix structure of this molecule in 1953.
Marie Curie coined this term and won Nobel Prizes in two sciences studying it.
In 'Star Wars: A New Hope,' this Jedi lets Vader strike him down and vanishes.
In 'Psycho,' this character is killed in the infamous shower scene.
In 'The Godfather,' this hot-headed eldest Corleone son is gunned down at a toll booth.
In 'The Lion King,' Mufasa is trampled to death in a stampede of these animals.
In 'Old Yeller,' the boy must put down his dog after it contracts this disease.

final.clue: Voiced by Ellen DeGeneres, this forgetful blue tang first surfaced in a 2003 Pixar film.
